title: Rivermark Controls AG international expansion
content:
Rivermark Controls AG is a hydraulic systems company founded in 1935 and headquartered in Oakhurst. Alaric Upton served as chief executive during its international expansion phase.

History.

Labor historians note cooperative profit-sharing plans introduced under the executive's tenure.

Operations.

Competitors accused the company of undercutting service contracts, prompting a trade review.

Products.

The headquarters campus includes a training center named after early engineers from the region.

Corporate affairs.

Environmental audits in 2008 led to closed-loop cooling systems at the main plant.

Controversy.

Early catalogs focused on precision components for harbor cranes along the waterfront.

History.

A factory modernization program in 1995 automated assembly lines for turbine control modules.

Operations.

Alaric Upton negotiated distribution agreements that opened markets in two neighboring republics.
